items.correct: Self-aware metafiction, intertextuality, scepticism toward grand narratives, playfulness with form, and blurring of high/low culture.
items.misconception: Postmodernism just means literature written after modernism.
items.random_wrong: Postmodernism means writing about the future.
items.why: Postmodernism is a specific set of attitudes and techniques, not a date range. A novel written in 2024 may be entirely traditional and not postmodern in any meaningful sense.

items.correct: A novel tracing a protagonist's moral and psychological development from youth to adulthood — typically involving formative experiences and a clearer sense of self.
items.misconception: Any novel with a young protagonist is a bildungsroman.
items.random_wrong: A bildungsroman is a novel about constructing buildings.
items.why: The focus must be on inward growth and development — having a young protagonist isn't enough. Students apply the term loosely to any story featuring a child or teenager.

items.q: What are conventions of the Gothic genre?
items.correct: Settings of ruin and decay, supernatural or psychological terror, transgression of boundaries, the sublime, doubled or haunted protagonists, and atmospheric excess.
items.misconception: Gothic just means scary stories with vampires and ghosts.
items.random_wrong: Gothic refers to medieval architecture, not literature.
items.why: Students reduce Gothic to monsters. The genre is defined by its concerns — boundaries, the past intruding on the present, the limits of reason — and only sometimes uses supernatural creatures.

items.q: Who gives the opening speech in Chapter 1?
items.why: Correct answer: Old Major. “Napoleon” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which animal is the loyal carthorse?
items.why: It's Boxer. “Mollie” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many Commandments are painted on the barn?
items.why: The real figure is 7. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
